name: canary_comment_only
description: comment text changed only - a behavioural suite must NOT fail on this
find: # Turn unwanted chars into the separator
replace: # Convert any unwanted chars into the separator

name: canary_equivalent_refactor
description: ordinalize() refactored via a local variable - same behaviour, new AST

name: ordinal_teens_boundary
description: ordinal() drops 12 from the 11/12/13 teens special case, so 12 -> '12nd'

name: ordinal_default_suffix
description: ordinal() default suffix becomes 'st', so 4 -> '4st' and 10 -> '10st'

name: camelize_lower_first
description: camelize(word, False) no longer lowercases an uppercase first letter

name: dasherize_first_only
description: dasherize() only replaces the first underscore

name: humanize_id_anywhere
description: humanize() strips '_id' anywhere in the word, not just as a trailing suffix

name: humanize_no_lowercase
description: humanize() no longer lowercases the rest of the word
find: word = re.sub(r"(?i)([a-z\d]*)", lambda m: m.group(1).lower(), word)
replace: word = re.sub(r"(?i)([a-z\d]*)", lambda m: m.group(1), word)

name: pluralize_bus_rule
description: pluralize() bus rule broken: 'bus' -> 'bues' instead of 'buses'

name: singularize_movies_rule
description: singularize() movies rule broken: 'movies' -> 'movi' instead of 'movie'

name: parameterize_squeeze_three
description: parameterize() only squeezes runs of 3+ separators; doubles survive

name: underscore_first_dash_only
description: underscore() only converts the first dash to an underscore

name: pluralize_empty_guard
description: pluralize() loses its empty-string guard, so '' -> 's'
find: if not word or word.lower() in UNCOUNTABLES:
